care_programs.name: Home Hospice Care
care_programs.short_description: Comprehensive hospice care delivered in the comfort of the patient’s home.
care_programs.long_description: Our Home Hospice Care program brings a multidisciplinary team directly to the patient’s home, focusing on comfort, dignity, and support for both patient and family. The team typically includes nurses, aides, social workers, chaplains, and volunteers who coordinate closely with the patient’s physician. Care plans are individualized and reviewed regularly to respond quickly to changing needs.
services_included: Regular nurse visits for assessment and symptom management
24/7 on-call nurse phone support
Hospice aide visits for personal care
Social work support for emotional and practical needs
Spiritual care visits on request
Home medical equipment and supplies related to the hospice diagnosis
Medication coordination and delivery
Care planning meetings with family

care_programs.name: Hospice Care
care_programs.short_description: Full-spectrum hospice services available at home and in our inpatient hospice units.
care_programs.long_description: Our Hospice Care program combines home-based services with access to inpatient hospice units when symptoms become difficult to manage at home. The interdisciplinary team partners with patients and families to honor goals and preferences, manage complex symptoms, and provide emotional, spiritual, and practical support. Flexible levels of care allow transitions between home and inpatient settings as needs change.
services_included: Frequent nurse visits for complex symptom management
24/7 nurse triage and urgent visit coordination
Hospice aide support several times per week
Access to inpatient hospice units for crisis or respite care
Medical director oversight and coordination with primary physicians
Social work, spiritual care, and volunteer support
Medication management and delivery
Durable medical equipment for home use

care_programs.name: Palliative Care at Home
care_programs.short_description: Home-based palliative care for serious illness alongside ongoing treatments.
care_programs.long_description: The Palliative Care at Home program supports patients living with serious illness who may still be receiving curative or life-prolonging treatments. Our team focuses on relief of pain and distressing symptoms, advance care planning, and helping patients and families clarify goals of care. Visits are typically less frequent than hospice but still provide regular touchpoints and 24/7 access to nurse phone support for urgent questions.
services_included: Scheduled home visits from palliative care nurses
24/7 nurse phone support for urgent concerns
Symptom assessment and medication recommendations
Support with complex medical decision-making
Social worker support for coping and resource navigation
Advance care planning and documentation support
Care coordination with specialists and primary care providers

care_programs.name: Clinic-Based Palliative Care
care_programs.short_description: Outpatient clinic visits focused on symptom relief and care planning.
care_programs.long_description: Clinic-Based Palliative Care offers appointments in our outpatient clinics for patients who prefer or require office-based visits. The palliative care team works alongside your existing specialists to address pain, shortness of breath, nausea, and other symptoms, while also supporting emotional well-being and advance care planning.
services_included: In-person visits with palliative care clinicians
Medication review and symptom management
Emotional and spiritual support resources
Advance care planning discussions
Coordination with oncology and other specialty teams

care_programs.name: Inpatient Hospice Unit
care_programs.short_description: Short-term inpatient hospice care for complex symptom management and respite.
care_programs.long_description: Our Inpatient Hospice Unit provides around-the-clock nursing care in a homelike setting for patients whose symptoms cannot be safely managed at home or who need brief respite stays. Families are encouraged to visit and participate in care. The environment is designed to support comfort, privacy, and meaningful time together.
services_included: 24-hour nursing and physician oversight
Intensive symptom management
Family support and overnight accommodations when available
Meals and personal care
On-site social work and chaplain services

care_programs.name: Pediatric Hospice Program
care_programs.short_description: Specialized hospice care for infants, children, and teens.
care_programs.long_description: The Pediatric Hospice Program provides compassionate, child-focused hospice care in the home and, when needed, in pediatric-capable inpatient settings. Our team supports the whole family, including siblings, with developmentally appropriate communication, play therapy, and grief support before and after a child’s death.
services_included: Pediatric nurse and physician oversight
Child life and play therapy support
Home hospice aide visits
Social work and spiritual care for the entire family
Siblings’ support and bereavement services
Care coordination with pediatric hospitals

care_programs.name: Bereavement Support Program
care_programs.short_description: Grief support for adults and families following a loss.
care_programs.long_description: Our Bereavement Support Program offers individual counseling, group support, and educational resources for adults and families grieving the death of a loved one. Services are available whether or not your loved one received care from our organization.
services_included: Short-term individual grief counseling
Facilitated grief support groups
Memorial events and remembrance services
Educational workshops on grief and coping

care_programs.name: Caregiver Coaching & Education
care_programs.short_description: Education and one-on-one coaching to support family caregivers.
care_programs.long_description: Caregiver Coaching & Education provides practical training, emotional support, and problem-solving assistance for family members caring for someone with serious illness. Sessions are offered virtually and focus on safe care techniques, communication with the health care team, and caregiver self-care.
services_included: One-on-one caregiver coaching sessions
Skill-building for safe transfers and personal care
Stress management and self-care strategies
Guidance on navigating health care systems

care_programs.name: Telehealth Symptom Management
care_programs.short_description: Video visits focused on managing pain and other symptoms at home.
care_programs.long_description: Telehealth Symptom Management offers video visits with palliative care clinicians to adjust medications, address new symptoms, and provide education without requiring travel to a clinic. This service can be used alone or alongside in-person hospice or palliative care programs.
services_included: Scheduled video visits with palliative clinicians
Medication review and adjustment
Education on non-medication approaches to symptom relief
Care coordination with home health or hospice teams

care_programs.name: Respite Care at Home
care_programs.short_description: Short-term in-home support so primary caregivers can rest.
care_programs.long_description: Respite Care at Home provides temporary in-home support from trained aides so that primary caregivers can take a break for rest, self-care, or other responsibilities. Respite visits can be scheduled regularly or on a short-term basis in coordination with your existing care team.
services_included: In-home aide support for personal care and supervision
Flexible scheduling based on caregiver needs
Basic household assistance related to patient care
Coordination with hospice or palliative teams when applicable

care_programs.name: Community Hospice Outreach Services
care_programs.short_description: Education and consultation for community providers and organizations.
care_programs.long_description: Community Hospice Outreach Services partners with clinics, nursing facilities, and community organizations to provide education about hospice and palliative care, early referral, and best practices for end-of-life care.
services_included: Educational presentations for health care teams
Consultation on complex end-of-life cases
Training on communication about goals of care

donation_funds.name: General Hospice & Palliative Care Fund
donation_funds.description: Supports the greatest needs across all hospice and palliative care programs, including uncompensated care, staff education, and new program development.

donation_funds.name: Pediatric Hospice Fund
donation_funds.description: Provides specialized services, equipment, and family support for children receiving hospice care and their siblings.

donation_funds.name: Bereavement Services Fund
donation_funds.description: Ensures that grief counseling and support groups remain available at low or no cost to the community.

donation_funds.name: Community Outreach & Education Fund
donation_funds.description: Supports community education, advance care planning workshops, and outreach to underserved populations.

facilities.name: San Francisco Inpatient Hospice Center
facilities.description: A dedicated inpatient hospice facility offering 24/7 nursing care, family-friendly rooms, and short-term stays for complex symptom management.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Daily visiting hours: 10:00 am – 8:00 pm (Monday–Friday) and 10:00 am – 6:00 pm (weekends). Quiet hours after 8:00 pm; children are welcome with adult supervision.

facilities.name: Mission Neighborhood Hospice House
facilities.description: A small, homelike inpatient hospice house located near the Mission District, with space for families to stay overnight.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Daily visiting hours: 9:00 am – 9:00 pm. Overnight stays for one family member may be arranged with the care team.

facilities.name: San Francisco Home Hospice Service Area
facilities.description: Our mobile team provides in-home hospice and palliative care across San Francisco, including the 94110 ZIP code.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Visits occur in the patient’s home; no on-site visiting hours. Nurses are available by phone 24/7 for urgent concerns.

facilities.name: San Francisco Outpatient Palliative Clinic
facilities.description: Clinic-based palliative care for adults receiving treatment at nearby hospitals and cancer centers.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Visitors may accompany patients to clinic appointments; please arrive 15 minutes early for check-in.

facilities.name: Chicago In-Home Hospice Team
facilities.description: Our Chicago In-home hospice team provides comprehensive In-home hospice services and palliative care at home for patients in and around ZIP code 60611.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: As an in-home service, there are no on-site visiting hours. Family and friends are welcome to visit in the patient’s home according to family preferences.

facilities.name: Chicago Inpatient Hospice Center
facilities.description: A modern inpatient hospice facility serving the greater Chicago area with private rooms and family spaces.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Daily visiting hours: 9:00 am – 9:00 pm. Children are welcome with an adult; quiet hours begin at 9:00 pm.

facilities.name: Atlanta Home Hospice Team
facilities.description: A multidisciplinary team providing home hospice care, pain and symptom management, and caregiver support throughout the metro Atlanta area, including ZIP code 30309.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Home hospice visits are scheduled with the family; visitors are welcome in the home at the family’s discretion.

facilities.name: Atlanta Inpatient Hospice Center
facilities.description: Inpatient hospice unit providing short-term stays for symptom stabilization and respite in midtown Atlanta.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Daily visiting hours: 8:00 am – 8:00 pm. Overnight visitors may be accommodated in select rooms; speak with the care team.

facilities.name: Denver Pediatric Hospice House
facilities.description: A pediatric-focused hospice house offering both inpatient care and coordination of in-home pediatric hospice services.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Daily visiting hours: 9:00 am – 8:00 pm, with flexible hours for parents and guardians. Sibling support spaces are available on-site.

facilities.name: Seattle Palliative Care Clinic
facilities.description: Outpatient clinic offering interdisciplinary palliative care consultations and follow-up visits.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Family members are welcome to attend clinic visits; please limit to two guests per patient due to space.

facilities.name: Boston Hospice Administrative Office
facilities.description: Administrative office for the Boston region hospice and palliative care teams.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: No patient care is provided at this location. Meetings are by appointment only.

facilities.name: Phoenix Home Hospice & Palliative Team
facilities.description: Provides in-home hospice and home-based palliative care across the greater Phoenix area.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: No on-site visiting; care is delivered in patient homes. 24/7 triage support is available by phone.

facilities.name: Miami Outpatient Palliative Center
facilities.description: Ambulatory palliative care center offering consultations, follow-up visits, and caregiver education.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Two visitors are allowed to accompany each patient. Please arrive 20 minutes early for first-time visits.

facilities.name: Austin Bereavement & Counseling Center
facilities.description: A hub for grief counseling, support groups, and memorial events for families in the Austin area.
facilities.visitor_information_summary: Support group participants should check in at the front desk. Doors open 30 minutes before scheduled group times.

guide_resources.title: End-of-Life Planning Guide for Family Caregivers (English/Spanish)
guide_resources.description: A bilingual (English/Spanish) printed guide to help family caregivers understand end-of-life options, make decisions, and prepare important documents.

guide_resources.title: Planning Ahead: A Patient’s Guide to End-of-Life Decisions
guide_resources.description: An English-language guide that explains advance directives, hospice and palliative care options, and how to talk with your health care team.

guide_resources.title: Caregiver Self-Care Toolkit
guide_resources.description: Tips, exercises, and checklists to help family caregivers manage stress and prevent burnout while caring for a loved one.

guide_resources.title: Your Grief Journey: Understanding Grief and Finding Support
guide_resources.description: A brochure that explains common grief reactions, coping strategies, and how to access individual and group support.

guide_resources.title: Managing Pain and Other Symptoms at Home
guide_resources.description: Plain-language explanations and practical tips for managing pain, shortness of breath, nausea, and other common hospice symptoms.

guide_resources.title: A Family Guide to Pediatric Hospice Care
guide_resources.description: Information for parents and caregivers about what to expect when a child is receiving hospice care, including support for siblings.

guide_resources.title: Guía de Directrices Médicas por Anticipado
guide_resources.description: A Spanish-language booklet explaining advance directives and how to document your wishes for medical care.

guide_resources.title: Hospice and Palliative Care: Frequently Asked Questions
guide_resources.description: Answers to common questions about eligibility, services, costs, and how hospice and palliative care work together.

support_groups.title: Weekly Online Support Group for Family Caregivers
support_groups.description: A facilitated online group for family caregivers to share experiences, coping strategies, and practical tips.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Register online to receive the recurring Zoom link and weekly reminder emails.

support_groups.title: Evening Check-In Group for Family Caregivers (Online)
support_groups.description: An informal weekly online group offering peer support and brief educational topics for adult family caregivers.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Complete the online form; you will receive a Zoom link and guidelines for participation.

support_groups.title: Midday Online Education Series for Family Caregivers
support_groups.description: A 4-session online series covering medication safety, fall prevention, and self-care for caregivers.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Register once to attend all four weekly sessions. Zoom link will be emailed after registration.

support_groups.title: Online Grief Support Group for Bereaved Caregivers
support_groups.description: A closed 8-week group for adults grieving the death of a spouse, partner, or close family member.
support_groups.registration_instructions: An intake call is required prior to joining. Please submit the online request form and a counselor will contact you.

support_groups.title: In-Person Caregiver Support Circle
support_groups.description: A drop-in support group for family caregivers, held at our Austin Bereavement & Counseling Center.
support_groups.registration_instructions: No registration required. Please arrive 10–15 minutes early to check in at the front desk.
support_groups.meeting_location: Austin Bereavement & Counseling Center, Group Room A

support_groups.title: Living with Advanced Illness: Symptom Management Group
support_groups.description: A hybrid group for patients living with advanced heart failure, lung disease, or cancer, focused on symptom relief and coping.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Speak with your palliative care clinician for a referral, then register online to choose in-person or Zoom attendance.
support_groups.meeting_location: San Francisco Outpatient Palliative Clinic or Online via Zoom

support_groups.title: Children & Teens Grief Group
support_groups.description: An activity-based group for children and teens ages 8–16 who are grieving the death of a loved one.
support_groups.registration_instructions: A parent or guardian must complete a brief intake call before the first session.
support_groups.meeting_location: Denver Pediatric Hospice House, Family Activity Room

support_groups.title: Advance Care Planning Workshop for the Community
support_groups.description: A one-time educational workshop to help adults complete advance directives and choose a health care proxy.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Register online to receive the Zoom link and downloadable forms.

support_groups.title: Morning Check-In for Family Caregivers (Online)
support_groups.description: A 6-week online series offering brief check-ins, mindfulness exercises, and resource sharing for caregivers.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Space is limited; please register online to reserve your spot and receive the Zoom link.

support_groups.title: Annual Day of Remembrance Retreat
support_groups.description: An all-day in-person retreat with ceremonies, workshops, and quiet reflection time for adults who are grieving.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Pre-registration is required; lunch and materials are provided. Scholarships are available.
support_groups.meeting_location: Austin Bereavement & Counseling Center, Main Hall

support_groups.title: Hospice 101: What Families Need to Know
support_groups.description: A live online presentation with Q&A about hospice eligibility, services, and how to get started.
support_groups.registration_instructions: Register on our website to receive the Zoom link and slides after the session.

facility_visiting_hours.notes: Daily visiting hours for Monday–Friday. Overnight stays for one family member may be arranged with the care team.
Weekend visiting hours. Please check with staff about children visiting.
Regular weekday visiting hours (Monday–Friday). Quiet hours begin at 20:00.
Weekend and holiday visiting hours. Limited services available after 18:00.
Daily visiting hours Monday–Friday. Children are welcome with an adult.
Weekend visiting hours. Quiet hours start at 21:00.
Standard weekday visiting hours. Overnight visitors may be accommodated in select rooms.
Weekend visiting hours. Please check in at the front desk on arrival.
Parents and guardians may visit at any time within these hours; siblings are welcome in designated family spaces.
Weekend visiting hours. Please coordinate group visits with the care team.
Drop-in hours for individual counseling and resource library. Support groups may meet later in the evening.
Open for scheduled groups and appointments only.
Closed on Sundays except for special events and memorial services.
Clinic hours for scheduled palliative care appointments. Please arrive 15 minutes early.
Family members may accompany patients; check-in closes at 16:30.
Regular clinic hours. Two visitors are allowed to accompany each patient.
Administrative office hours; meetings are by appointment only. No patient care provided on-site.
